data.title: Blackstone Closes $6.3 Billion Life Sciences Fund
data.description:
By Nicholas G. Miller

Blackstone closed a $6.3 billion life sciences fund, which it said is the largest life sciences private fund ever raised.

The company said the fund was oversubscribed and closed at its cap.

Blackstone launched its life sciences platform, which has $15 billion in assets under management as of the fourth quarter of last year, in 2018 to invest across companies and products in life science sectors.

Write to Nicholas G. Miller at nicholas.miller@wsj.com.

data.title: Trio Petroleum updates ATM prospectus; $1.01M placement shares remain, $20.03M max offering
data.description:
Trio Petroleum filed successive prospectus amendments under its ATM program, leaving $1,010,000 of Placement Shares available and a $20,028,000 maximum aggregate offering.

Key Highlights:

  • Entered an At Market Issuance Agreement with Ladenburg Thalmann to sell common stock under an S-3 registration.
  • As of March 30, 2026, $1,010,000 of Placement Shares remain available for sale under Instruction I.B.6.
  • Maximum aggregate offering amount increased to $20,028,000, reflecting $19,016,726 previously sold (19,202,455 shares).
  • Legal opinion from Ellenoff Grossman & Schole LLP for the Placement Shares filed as Exhibit 5.1.

Original SEC Filing: Trio Petroleum Corp [ TPET ] - 8-K - Mar. 30, 2026

data.title: SS Innovations gains regulatory approval for SSi Mantra in Colombia, Oman, Sri Lanka and Kenya
data.description:
SS Innovations received regulatory approval for the SSi Mantra surgical robotic system in Colombia, Oman, Sri Lanka and Kenya.

Key Highlights:

  • Received approvals: INVIMA (Colombia) Nov 2025; DGPA&DC (Oman) Nov 2025; NMRA (Sri Lanka) Jan 2026; PPB (Kenya) Jan 2026.
  • SSi Mantra now approved in eleven countries, expanding addressable underpenetrated markets for the company.
  • Cumulative installed base of 168 systems across ten countries and 7,885 surgeries as of Dec 31, 2025.
  • Over 150 telesurgeries performed; product features and lower cost position it for adoption in new jurisdictions.

Original SEC Filing: SS Innovations International, Inc. [ SSII ] - 8-K - Mar. 30, 2026

data.title: Energy Focus reports $6.6M Project Y data center contract and completed $0.5M Project G
data.description:
Energy Focus announced completion of Project G and a multi-year $6.6 million Project Y data center deployment.

Key Highlights:

  • Completed Project G in 2025, a large-scale UPS installation that contributed ~$0.5M to 2025 revenue.
  • Advancing multi-year Project Y (2026–2027) with estimated contract value of ~$6.6M for large-capacity UPS and cooling.
  • Project Y scope includes UPS units (250kW–1250kW) and high-efficiency Fan Wall Units for phased data center expansion.
  • Engagements expand footprint in AI-ready, large-scale data center markets and reinforce infrastructure delivery capabilities.

data.title: Euro Stays Weaker After German Inflation Data — Market Talk
data.description: The euro remains weaker against the dollar, showing little reaction after data showed German inflation accelerated in line with expectations in March. Harmonized inflation rose to 2.8% in March from 2.0% in February, according to provisional data. Inflation was expected to rise following a sharp increase in oil prices stemming from the Iran war. The market is pricing in a 63% chance that the European Central Bank will respond by raising interest rates in April with 72 basis points of rate increased expected by year-end, LSEG data show. The euro falls 0.3% to $1.1477, little changed from levels before the data. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

data.title: Royalty Pharma, Johnson & Johnson in $500 Million Autoimmune Drug Funding Pact
data.description:
By Colin Kellaher

Royalty Pharma has inked a $500 million research-and-development co-funding agreement with Johnson & Johnson to support development of a proposed drug for autoimmune diseases.

Royalty Pharma, a New York biopharmaceutical royalties investor, on Monday said it will provide the funding in 2026 and 2027 to advance the development of Johnson & Johnson's JNJ-4804 drug candidate.

JNJ-4804 is currently in Phase 2 studies for the autoimmune diseases ulcerative colitis, psoriatic arthritis and Crohn's disease.

New Brunswick, N.J., pharmaceutical giant J&J last month struck a deal under which asset manager Blackstone will help fund the development of its bleximenib drug candidate for the blood cancer acute myeloid leukemia.

Write to Colin Kellaher at colin.kellaher@wsj.com

data.title: European Powers Urge Israel To Drop Death Penalty Expansion Bill
data.description:
PARIS (dpa-AFX) - Four major European powers have urged the decision makers in the Israeli parliament and Government to abandon plans to expand the use of death penalty in a Bill that could be voted into law next week.

In a joint statement issued on Sunday, the Foreign Ministers of Germany, France, Italy and the United Kingdom expressed deep concern about the de facto discriminatory character of the bill.

The adoption of this bill would risk undermining Israel's commitments with regards to democratic principles, according to them.

'The death penalty is an inhumane and degrading form of punishment without any deterring effect. This is why we oppose the death penalty, whatever the circumstances around the world. The rejection of the death penalty is a fundamental value that unites us,' the statement says.

Also Sunday, Council of Europe chief Alain Berset appealed to the Israeli government to repeal the draft law, which is set to be read in the Knesset on Monday.

Copyright(c) 2026 RTTNews.com. All Rights Reserved

Copyright RTT News/dpa-AFX

data.title: Wall Street brokerages stick to two Fed rate cuts in 2026
data.description:
Major brokerages are still forecasting two U.S. Federal Reserve interest-rate cuts in 2026 in contrast to the central bank's latest projections, which signaled a single quarter-point reduction amid renewed inflation concerns fueled by the Middle East war.

Nomura joined peers Goldman Sachs and Barclays to expect the first rate reduction in September, compared with June previously.

The Fed left rates unchanged at its meeting in March, as expected. Investors focused on a cautious tone by Chair Jerome Powell - that the economic outlook remains uncertain amid the Iran war.

Money market participants are no longer pricing in any easing from the U.S. central bank this year, compared to two rate cuts before the conflict erupted, according to the CME Group's FedWatch Tool.

data.title: Keyera's Buy of Plains' Canada NGL Business No Longer Expected to Be Completed This Quarter
data.description:
By Robb M. Stewart

Keyera's acquisition of Plains All American Pipeline's natural gas liquids business in Canada is taking longer than anticipated to clear regulatory hurdles.

The energy-infrastructure company said Monday the proposed 5.15 billion Canadian dollar (US$3.71 billion) deal continues to advance through the regulatory process, and the transaction is no longer expected to close by the end of the first quarter as previously expected.

Based on the current status of the regulatory process, Keyera said it is now working toward closing the transaction in May.

President and Chief Executive Dean Setoguchi said the company remains confident the deal is in the best interest of industry, Keyera and Canada.

Keyera in June unveiled the move to boost its scale and widen its reach into Eastern Canada with an agreement to buy substantially all of Plains' natural gas liquids business in Canada, along with select U.S. assets.

The takeover, the largest Keyera has agreed to date, would establish a natural gas liquids corridor in Canada for the company, with assets that include extraction, fractionation and storage operations, as well as rail and truck terminals in Alberta, Saskatchewan, Manitoba and Ontario. The company has said the move would allow it to be more competitive in the services it offers and in terms of reliability, while bringing an opportunity to cut costs. Setoguchi at the time said the deal also would repatriate important energy assets in Canada and mean that cash flows from the acquired operations will reinvested in the country.

Write to Robb M. Stewart at robb.stewart@wsj.com

data.title: Exchange Income to Renew 10% Share-Repurchase Program
data.description:
By Adriano Marchese

Exchange Income intends to renew its share-repurchase program allowing the company to buy back up to 10% of its issued and outstanding stock over the course of a year.

The diversified acquisition-driven holding company said Monday that the Toronto Stock Exchange has approved its plan to buy back up to 5.31 million shares for cancellation by way of a normal course issuer bid.

Shares have been performing well in 2026, rising about 25% since the start of the year to close on Friday at 102.26 Canadian dollars. The stock has roughly doubled over the past 52 weeks.

At Friday's closing price, the value of the shares intended for buyback would be worth about C$543.2 million.

Exchange Income can begin buying back shares starting on April 1.

Write to Adriano Marchese at adriano.marchese@wsj.com
